description:
You are a web-developer who was handed an existing configuration file and you need to give the QA team a **clean, human-readable summary of the database connection parameters** embedded in it.

Initial conditions available in the container
---------------------------------------------
1. The file “/home/user/webapp/config/settings.ini” already exists.  
2. It is a standard INI file that contains several sections such as “[server]”, “[email]”, “[database]”, “[feature_flags]”, etc.

What you must do
----------------
1. Read **only** the values located in the **[database]** section of “/home/user/webapp/config/settings.ini”.  
2. Create a directory called “/home/user/webapp/reports” (if it does not already exist).  
3. Inside that directory, create a text file named **db_connection.log** that follows *exactly* the template shown below.

Required template for /home/user/webapp/reports/db_connection.log
-----------------------------------------------------------------
The file must contain **five lines** and **five lines only**:

LINE 1: DATABASE CONNECTION DETAILS  
LINE 2: host=<value copied from settings.ini>  
LINE 3: port=<value copied from settings.ini>  
LINE 4: user=<value copied from settings.ini>  
LINE 5: password=<value copied from settings.ini>  

Further formatting requirements:
• Use lowercase key names (host, port, user, password) exactly as shown.  
• There must be **one single trailing newline** at the end of the fifth line (no extra blank lines).  
• No additional whitespace is allowed before or after the “=” sign.  
• Do not include any comments or other keys from the INI file.

4. After the file is written, print the path “/home/user/webapp/reports/db_connection.log” to STDOUT so that the automated grader can easily discover it.

Hints
-----
• For INI files you can use tools such as `grep`, `awk`, or `python -c "import configparser …"`—whatever you prefer.  
• Keep the solution short (roughly 3–4 shell commands).  
• You do not need root privileges; everything lives in “/home/user”.  

The grader will check
---------------------
1. That “/home/user/webapp/reports/db_connection.log” exists.  
2. That its **exact** content (bytes, newlines, order) matches the template above with the correct values extracted from “[database]” in the INI file.  
3. That exactly one trailing newline follows the final line.  
4. That the agent printed the absolute path to STDOUT after creation.

If any of those verifications fail, the task will be marked incorrect. Good luck!
truth:
File that exists before the task starts
---------------------------------------
Path: /home/user/webapp/config/settings.ini

Exact byte-for-byte contents (including newlines):

[server]
host = 0.0.0.0
port = 8080

[email]
smtp_host = mail.example.com
smtp_port = 587
username = notify@example.com
password = mailingR0cks!

[database]
host = 172.16.24.10
port = 5432
user = webapp
password = s3cr3t!

[feature_flags]
dark_mode = true
beta_payments = false


Expected file after the agent finishes
---------------------------------------
Path: /home/user/webapp/reports/db_connection.log

Exact required content (including the single trailing newline character):

DATABASE CONNECTION DETAILS
host=172.16.24.10
port=5432
user=webapp
password=s3cr3t!
name: task_000000_72d0904b
